title: 1.1: Tests that executeQuery() returns a single data frame with frame name 'test' and 'Time' as first field name when executing a basic query with verifyGoldenResponse helper
1.2: Tests that executeQuery() returns exactly 3 data frames with 2 labels preserved on value fields when processing multiple tables from query response
1.4: Tests that executeQuery() raises error containing 'aggregateWindow()' recommendation when data point limit is exceeded without aggregation in query
1.5: Tests that executeQuery() raises error without 'aggregateWindow()' recommendation when data point limit is exceeded with aggregation already present in query
2.1: Places the test validating basic query execution structure in pkg/tsdb/influxdb/flux/executor_test.go
2.2: Places the test validating multiple data frame label preservation in pkg/tsdb/influxdb/flux/executor_test.go
2.3: Places the test validating aggregated query pointer-backed fields in pkg/tsdb/influxdb/flux/executor_test.go
2.4: Places the test validating data point limit error without aggregation in pkg/tsdb/influxdb/flux/executor_test.go
2.5: Places the test validating data point limit error with aggregation in pkg/tsdb/influxdb/flux/executor_test.go
3.1: All tests use Go testing framework with testify require package for assertions
3.2: All tests follow the naming pattern Test<Feature><Scenario> using PascalCase
3.3: All tests use helper functions (verifyGoldenResponse or executeMockedQuery) to execute queries before making assertions
4.1: Follows pattern of using require.Len(t, dr.Frames, N) to verify frame count for test cases validating data frame responses
4.2: Follows pattern of using require.EqualError(t, dr.Error, expectedMessage) to verify exact error message content for test cases validating error conditions
4.3: Follows pattern of using cmp.Diff with data.FrameTestCompareOptions() for deep frame comparison in test cases verifying exact frame content
